after.description: Tests failing: Unit T216, T217, Integration T42, T43. after.title: Fix file locking concurrency tests
after.description: Tests failing: Unit T274, T279, T280, T283. Tests: concurrent lock timeout, lock released on error, lock timeout configurable, race condition prevention. Root cause: timing-sensitive tests that depend on system load and flock behavior. May need test isolation or mocking. See: lib/file-ops.sh lock_file() unlock_file()

before.title: Create unified lib/backup.sh library
before.description: Phase 1 of unified backup architecture. Create centralized backup library with: create_backup(type, file, options), restore_backup(backup_id, options), rotate_backups(type, max), list_backups(type), get_backup_metadata(backup_id). Types: snapshot, safety, incremental, archive, migration. See: claudedocs/BACKUP-ARCHITECTURE.md for full spec.

before.title: Implement backup type taxonomy and directory structure
before.description: New directory structure: .claude/backups/snapshot/, .claude/backups/safety/, .claude/backups/incremental/, .claude/backups/archive/, .claude/backups/migration/. Each backup includes metadata JSON with timestamp, type, source, checksum, description. See: claudedocs/BACKUP-ARCHITECTURE.md unified directory structure.

before.title: Add legacy backup migration command
before.description: Phase 3 of unified architecture. Create 'claude-todo backup migrate-legacy' command to move old backups from .claude/.backups/ to new .claude/backups/ structure. Classify by age/type, generate metadata. See: claudedocs/BACKUP-ARCHITECTURE.md Phase 3.

before.title: P0: Fix task ID collision under concurrent operations
before.description: File locking exists but ID generation happens BEFORE lock acquisition. Under concurrent load (5 parallel adds), all processes generate same ID causing 80% data loss. Solution: Move ID generation inside locked critical section. See claudedocs/P0-1-RACE-CONDITION-ISSUE.md for full root cause analysis, fix requirements, and implementation plan.

after.title: P0: Fix task ID collision under concurrent operations

before.title: Add dependency check to install.sh before installation
before.description: install.sh should check for required dependencies (jq, bash 4+) BEFORE installing. Currently installs without checking, leaving user with broken install. Add check_dependencies() function that: 1) Checks jq exists, 2) Checks bash version >= 4.0, 3) Provides platform-specific install instructions, 4) Optionally offers to install deps (apt/brew/yum).

after.title: Add dependency check to install.sh before installation
after.description: install.sh should check for required dependencies (jq, bash 4+) BEFORE installing. Currently installs without checking, leaving user with broken install. Add check_dependencies() function that: 1) Checks jq exists, 2) Checks bash version >= 4.0, 3) Provides platform-specific install instructions, 4) Optionally offers to install deps (apt/brew/yum).

before.description: install.sh should check for required dependencies (jq, bash 4+) BEFORE installing. Currently installs without checking, leaving user with broken install. after.title: Add dependency check to install.sh before installation
after.description: Add comprehensive dependency check to install.sh before installation begins. This is the master dependency validation that calls individual checks for: jq, bash 4+, sha256sum/shasum, tar, flock, numfmt, date/find. Must run BEFORE any files are copied. Provides clear error messages with platform-specific install instructions (apt/brew/yum/dnf). Exit code 1 if critical deps missing.

before.title: Phase 1: Evidence Collection - Establish ground truth before analysis
before.description: Foundation phase: Read all docs, analyze codebase, reproduce bugs, extract metrics. Must complete before specialist analysis begins. Exit criteria: All 4 doc files indexed, lib/*.sh analyzed, bug reproduction tests run, baseline metrics captured.

after.title: Phase 1: Evidence Collection - Establish ground truth before analysis
after.description: Foundation phase: Read all docs, analyze codebase, reproduce bugs, extract metrics. Must complete before specialist analysis begins. Exit criteria: All 4 doc files indexed, lib/*.sh analyzed, bug reproduction tests run, baseline metrics captured.

before.title: Phase 2: Specialist Analysis - 5 parallel domain investigations
before.description: Deploy 5 specialist agents for deep domain analysis. Each agent owns specific claims and produces evidence dossiers. Agents: Technical Validation, Design Philosophy, Documentation, Implementation, Challenge (Red Team). All run in parallel after Phase 1 gate passes.

after.title: Phase 2: Specialist Analysis - 5 parallel domain investigations
after.description: Deploy 5 specialist agents for deep domain analysis. Each agent owns specific claims and produces evidence dossiers. Agents: Technical Validation, Design Philosophy, Documentation, Implementation, Challenge (Red Team). All run in parallel after Phase 1 gate passes.

before.title: Phase 3: Challenge & Consensus - Round-robin adversarial validation
before.description: Adversarial validation phase. ROUND 1: Each agent presents findings (max 200 tokens/claim with evidence citations). ROUND 2: Cross-examination (each agent challenges 2 others, format: 'Claim X assumes Y, but evidence Z contradicts'). ROUND 3: Consensus voting (PROVEN: 4/5 + reproducible evidence, REFUTED: counter-evidence invalidates, CONTESTED: 3/5 split). Gate: All 10 claims have verdict.

after.title: Phase 3: Challenge & Consensus - Round-robin adversarial validation
after.description: Adversarial validation phase. ROUND 1: Each agent presents findings (max 200 tokens/claim with evidence citations). ROUND 2: Cross-examination (each agent challenges 2 others, format: 'Claim X assumes Y, but evidence Z contradicts'). ROUND 3: Consensus voting (PROVEN: 4/5 + reproducible evidence, REFUTED: counter-evidence invalidates, CONTESTED: 3/5 split). Gate: All 10 claims have verdict.

before.title: Phase 4: Synthesis - Generate final artifacts and recommendations
before.description: Final output generation. ARTIFACTS: 1) Consensus Report (methodology, proven/refuted/contested claims, recommendations), 2) Feature Specs (for validated gaps like auto-archive, exists cmd), 3) Documentation Corrections (diffs for inaccurate claims), 4) Evidence Dossiers (per-claim transparency archive). Gate: All artifacts published, evidence archived.

after.title: Phase 4: Synthesis - Generate final artifacts and recommendations
after.description: Final output generation. ARTIFACTS: 1) Consensus Report (methodology, proven/refuted/contested claims, recommendations), 2) Feature Specs (for validated gaps like auto-archive, exists cmd), 3) Documentation Corrections (diffs for inaccurate claims), 4) Evidence Dossiers (per-claim transparency archive). Gate: All artifacts published, evidence archived.

before.title: FEATURE: Multi-Phase Consensus Research Framework Plugin
before.description:
Build a reusable plugin/command for claude-todo that implements the Multi-Phase Consensus Research Framework. This would be a programmatically callable tool with prompt-style instructions for any project to use when investigating claims, validating features, or planning complex work.

## CONCEPT
A repeatable, structured process for rigorous multi-agent investigation with:
- Configurable phases (Discovery  Adversarial  Consolidation  Validation  Final)
- Agent role definitions (Advocate/Challenger/Arbiter pattern)
- Gate-based progression (scope approval, conflict resolution, archive approval, completeness sign-off)
- Evidence standards and consensus thresholds
- Artifact generation (reports, specs, corrections)

## PROPOSED STRUCTURE
| Round | Purpose | Agents | Gate |
|-------|---------|--------|------|
| Round 1 | Discovery & Inventory | 6 inventory agents | Gate 1: Scope approval |
| Round 2 | Adversarial Challenge | 15 (5 domains  Advocate/Challenger/Arbiter) | Gate 2: Conflict resolution |
| Round 3 | Consolidation | 7 merge agents | Gate 3: Archive approval |
| Round 4 | Validation | 3 fresh validation agents | Gate 4: Completeness sign-off |
| Round 5 | Final Spec | 2 finalization agents | Gate 5: Implementation GO |

## INVOCATION
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus \
  --claims 'claim1.md,claim2.md' \
  --evidence-dir ./evidence \
  --output-dir ./consensus-output \
  --agents 5 \
  --threshold 0.8
```

## KEY FEATURES
1. Prompt-driven agent deployment (subagent_type patterns)
2. Round-robin challenge protocol with configurable rounds
3. Evidence standards (reproducible tests, measurements, citations)
4. Consensus thresholds (4/5 default, configurable)
5. Artifact templates (report, feature spec, doc correction, evidence dossier)
6. Gate progression with approval checkpoints
7. Deadlock resolution mechanisms
8. Context handoff for long investigations

## IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Could be a Claude Code plugin or claude-todo subcommand
- Needs integration with Task tool for agent deployment
- Should support both interactive and batch modes
- Evidence dossiers should be machine-readable (JSON) and human-readable (MD)
- Consider Serena MCP integration for code analysis phases

## SUCCESS CRITERIA
- Repeatable: Same inputs produce consistent process
- Valuable output: Single consolidated report, not scattered docs
- Configurable: Adjust agents, rounds, thresholds per project
- Transparent: Full evidence trail for all decisions

before.title: P1.1: Document corpus indexing - Read TODO_Task_Management.md, usage.md, CLAUDE.md
before.description: Read and index key documentation files that govern LLM behavior with claude-todo. Files: docs/TODO_Task_Management.md (primary LLM instructions), docs/usage.md (user-facing docs), CLAUDE.md (project-level injected instructions). Identify contradictions, gaps, and claims to validate.

after.title: P1.1: Document corpus indexing - Read TODO_Task_Management.md, usage.md, CLAUDE.md
after.description: Read and index key documentation files that govern LLM behavior with claude-todo. Files: docs/TODO_Task_Management.md (primary LLM instructions), docs/usage.md (user-facing docs), CLAUDE.md (project-level injected instructions). Identify contradictions, gaps, and claims to validate.

before.title: P1.2: Implementation reality map - Analyze lib/*.sh and scripts/*.sh via Serena
before.description: Use Serena MCP to analyze all library and script files. Map functions, their purposes, actual behaviors vs documented behaviors. Key files: lib/file-ops.sh (atomic writes, locking), lib/logging.sh, lib/validation.sh, lib/backup.sh, lib/output-format.sh. Identify feature existence vs claims.

after.title: P1.2: Implementation reality map - Analyze lib/*.sh and scripts/*.sh via Serena
after.description: Use Serena MCP to analyze all library and script files. Map functions, their purposes, actual behaviors vs documented behaviors. Key files: lib/file-ops.sh (atomic writes, locking), lib/logging.sh, lib/validation.sh, lib/backup.sh, lib/output-format.sh. Identify feature existence vs claims.

before.title: P2.3: Documentation Agent - cross-reference audit, LLM instruction effectiveness
before.description: OWNS CLAIMS: CLI-only rule doesn't prevent ID hallucinations, JSON reads should be allowed. DELIVERABLE: Consistency audit, CLI-only evaluation, proposed doc revisions.

after.title: P2.3: Documentation Agent - cross-reference audit, LLM instruction effectiveness
after.description: OWNS CLAIMS: CLI-only rule doesn't prevent ID hallucinations, JSON reads should be allowed. A repeatable, structured process for rigorous multi-agent investigation with:
- Configurable phases (Discovery  Adversarial  Consolidation  Validation  Final)
- Agent role definitions (Advocate/Challenger/Arbiter pattern)
- Gate-based progression (scope approval, conflict resolution, archive approval, completeness sign-off)
- Evidence standards and consensus thresholds
- Artifact generation (reports, specs, corrections)

## PROPOSED STRUCTURE
| Round | Purpose | Agents | Gate |
|-------|---------|--------|------|
| Round 1 | Discovery & Inventory | 6 inventory agents | Gate 1: Scope approval |
| Round 2 | Adversarial Challenge | 15 (5 domains  Advocate/Challenger/Arbiter) | Gate 2: Conflict resolution |
| Round 3 | Consolidation | 7 merge agents | Gate 3: Archive approval |
| Round 4 | Validation | 3 fresh validation agents | Gate 4: Completeness sign-off |
| Round 5 | Final Spec | 2 finalization agents | Gate 5: Implementation GO |

## INVOCATION
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus \
  --claims 'claim1.md,claim2.md' \
  --evidence-dir ./evidence \
  --output-dir ./consensus-output \
  --agents 5 \
  --threshold 0.8
```

## KEY FEATURES
1. Prompt-driven agent deployment (subagent_type patterns)
2. Round-robin challenge protocol with configurable rounds
3. Evidence standards (reproducible tests, measurements, citations)
4. Consensus thresholds (4/5 default, configurable)
5. Artifact templates (report, feature spec, doc correction, evidence dossier)
6. Gate progression with approval checkpoints
7. Deadlock resolution mechanisms
8. Context handoff for long investigations

## IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Could be a Claude Code plugin or claude-todo subcommand
- Needs integration with Task tool for agent deployment
- Should support both interactive and batch modes
- Evidence dossiers should be machine-readable (JSON) and human-readable (MD)
- Consider Serena MCP integration for code analysis phases

## SUCCESS CRITERIA
- Repeatable: Same inputs produce consistent process
- Valuable output: Single consolidated report, not scattered docs
- Configurable: Adjust agents, rounds, thresholds per project
- Transparent: Full evidence trail for all decisions

before.title: T204.1: Configuration Schema Design
before.description:
Design YAML/JSON configuration schema for reusable consensus framework plugin.

## DELIVERABLE
`schemas/consensus-research-config.schema.json`

## SCHEMA MUST DEFINE
- claims: Array of claim definitions (id, title, description, category)
- evidence_sources: Paths to evidence directories/files
- agents: Agent configuration (count, types, subagent_type mappings)
- thresholds: Consensus thresholds (proven, refuted, contested)
- output: Output directory and file naming conventions
- gates: HITL gate configuration (which gates, timeout, blocking)
- phases: Phase definitions (which phases to run, order)

## EXAMPLE CONFIG
```yaml
framework: consensus-research
version: 1.0
investigation:
  name: "claude-todo-claims"
  claims_file: ./claims.yaml
  evidence_dir: ./evidence
  output_dir: ./consensus-output
agents:
  worker_count: 5
  synthesis_type: project-supervisor-orchestrator
thresholds:
  proven: 0.8  # 4/5
  refuted: 0.4  # 2/5
gates:
  - after_phase: 1
    blocking: true
    timeout: 24h
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Execution Protocol section)
- T204 parent task for context

before.title: T204.2: Parameterized Prompt Templates
before.description:
Create parameterized prompt templates for all 6 agent types.

## DELIVERABLE
`templates/consensus-research/` directory with:
- agent-technical.prompt.md
- agent-design.prompt.md
- agent-docs.prompt.md
- agent-impl.prompt.md
- agent-challenge.prompt.md
- agent-synthesis.prompt.md

## TEMPLATE VARIABLES
Each template must use placeholders:
- `{INVESTIGATION_NAME}` - Name of the investigation
- `{CLAIM_SET}` - List of claims assigned to this agent
- `{EVIDENCE_PATH}` - Path to phase 1 evidence file
- `{OUTPUT_PATH}` - Path where agent writes findings
- `{EVIDENCE_STANDARDS}` - Standards for this claim type
- `{CONSENSUS_THRESHOLD}` - Voting threshold
- `{OTHER_AGENT_OUTPUTS}` - Paths to other agent files (for synthesis)

## EXAMPLE TEMPLATE
```markdown
# {AGENT_ROLE} Agent

You are the {AGENT_ROLE} for the {INVESTIGATION_NAME} investigation.

## Your Assigned Claims
{CLAIM_SET}

## Evidence Baseline
Read evidence from: {EVIDENCE_PATH}

## Your Task
Investigate your assigned claims using {EVIDENCE_STANDARDS}.

## Output
Write findings to: {OUTPUT_PATH}

## Output Format
[structured template...]
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Agent Roles, Evidence Passing Protocol)

before.title: T204.3: State Management Design
before.description:
Design state management system for tracking investigation progress.

## DELIVERABLE
`schemas/consensus-research-state.schema.json` + implementation spec

## STATE FILE MUST TRACK
- current_phase: Which phase is active (1-4)
- phase_status: Per-phase completion status
- claims: Per-claim status and verdicts
- agents: Which agents have completed, their output paths
- gates: Gate status (pending, approved, rejected)
- questions: Collected questions with severity and status
- suspicious_consensus: Flagged unanimous agreements
- session: Current session ID, start time, last activity

## STATE TRANSITIONS
```
Phase 1: pending  active  evidence_collected  gate_1_pending  gate_1_approved
Phase 2: pending  agents_spawned  agents_complete  gate_2_pending  gate_2_approved
Phase 3: pending  synthesis_active  voting_complete  gate_3_pending  gate_3_approved
Phase 4: pending  artifacts_generating  complete  gate_4_approved
```

## PERSISTENCE
- State file: `.consensus-research/state.json`
- Atomic writes (temp file  validate  rename)
- Checksum for integrity
- Recovery from interrupted state

## INTEGRATION WITH CLAUDE-TODO
- Optionally create claude-todo tasks from state
- Sync state  task status
- Export state to TodoWrite format

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Completion Criteria, Position Identification)

before.title: T204.6: Error Recovery Protocols
before.description:
Define error handling and recovery protocols for framework robustness.

## DELIVERABLE
Error recovery section in spec + state recovery implementation

## ERROR SCENARIOS

### Agent Failure
- Worker agent returns error or incomplete output
- Mitigation: Retry with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts)
- Fallback: Mark claim as INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE, flag for HITL

### HITL Gate Timeout
- User doesn't respond within timeout period
- Mitigation: Reminder notification, extend timeout
- Fallback: Auto-proceed with Synthesis Agent recommendation (if configured)
- Alternative: Pause investigation, persist state for resume

### Session Interruption
- Context window exhausted mid-investigation
- Mitigation: State file persists all progress
- Recovery: New session reads state, resumes from last checkpoint
- Phase checkpoints: After each phase completion

### Conflicting Agent Outputs
- Two agents give contradictory evidence for same claim
- Mitigation: Challenge Agent reviews conflict
- Escalation: SUSPICIOUS_CONSENSUS or HITL gate

### Missing Evidence
- Phase 1 cannot find sufficient evidence for claims
- Mitigation: Flag claims as EVIDENCE_NEEDED
- Options: Proceed with available evidence, request more sources, abort claim

### State Corruption
- State file corrupted or invalid
- Mitigation: Checksum validation on read
- Recovery: Reconstruct from output files + claude-todo tasks
- Backup: Periodic state snapshots

## RECOVERY COMMANDS
```bash
claude-todo research --resume          # Resume from state file
claude-todo research --status          # Show current state
claude-todo research --recover         # Attempt state reconstruction
claude-todo research --abort           # Clean abort with state preservation
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Risk Mitigation, Session Recovery)

before.title: T204.7: Invocation Modes Specification
before.description:
Define different invocation modes for the framework.

## DELIVERABLE
CLI specification section with all modes documented

## INVOCATION MODES

### Interactive Mode (Default)
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus --config ./research.yaml
```
- Pauses at each HITL gate for user input
- Shows progress in real-time
- Allows mid-investigation adjustments

### Batch Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus --config ./research.yaml --batch
```
- Runs without HITL pauses (uses Synthesis Agent recommendations)
- Collects all questions for end-of-run review
- Suitable for overnight/unattended runs

### Dry Run Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus --config ./research.yaml --dry-run
```
- Validates config, shows execution plan
- Lists claims, agents, phases, gates
- No actual agent spawning

### Resume Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --resume [state-file]
```
- Resumes from persisted state
- Validates state integrity before resuming
- Shows what was completed, what remains

### Status Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --status [state-file]
```
- Shows current investigation status
- Phase progress, claim verdicts, pending gates
- No modification

### Single Phase Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --phase 2 --config ./research.yaml
```
- Run only specific phase
- Requires prior phase outputs to exist
- Useful for re-running failed phases

## CLI OPTIONS
| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| --config | Path to config YAML |
| --framework | Framework type (consensus, adversarial, etc.) |
| --batch | Run without HITL pauses |
| --dry-run | Validate and show plan only |
| --resume | Resume from state file |
| --status | Show investigation status |
| --phase N | Run specific phase only |
| --verbose | Detailed output |
| --output-dir | Override output directory |

## REFERENCES
- T204 parent task
- claude-todo existing CLI patterns

before.title: T204.1: Configuration Schema Design
before.description:
Design YAML/JSON configuration schema for reusable consensus framework plugin.

## DELIVERABLE
`schemas/consensus-research-config.schema.json`

## SCHEMA MUST DEFINE
- claims: Array of claim definitions (id, title, description, category)
- evidence_sources: Paths to evidence directories/files
- agents: Agent configuration (count, types, subagent_type mappings)
- thresholds: Consensus thresholds (proven, refuted, contested)
- output: Output directory and file naming conventions
- gates: HITL gate configuration (which gates, timeout, blocking)
- phases: Phase definitions (which phases to run, order)

## EXAMPLE CONFIG
```yaml
framework: consensus-research
version: 1.0
investigation:
  name: "claude-todo-claims"
  claims_file: ./claims.yaml
  evidence_dir: ./evidence
  output_dir: ./consensus-output
agents:
  worker_count: 5
  synthesis_type: project-supervisor-orchestrator
thresholds:
  proven: 0.8  # 4/5
  refuted: 0.4  # 2/5
gates:
  - after_phase: 1
    blocking: true
    timeout: 24h
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Execution Protocol section)
- T204 parent task for context

- agent-impl.prompt.md
- agent-challenge.prompt.md
- agent-synthesis.prompt.md

## TEMPLATE VARIABLES
Each template must use placeholders:
- `{INVESTIGATION_NAME}` - Name of the investigation
- `{CLAIM_SET}` - List of claims assigned to this agent
- `{EVIDENCE_PATH}` - Path to phase 1 evidence file
- `{OUTPUT_PATH}` - Path where agent writes findings
- `{EVIDENCE_STANDARDS}` - Standards for this claim type
- `{CONSENSUS_THRESHOLD}` - Voting threshold
- `{OTHER_AGENT_OUTPUTS}` - Paths to other agent files (for synthesis)

## EXAMPLE TEMPLATE
```markdown
# {AGENT_ROLE} Agent

You are the {AGENT_ROLE} for the {INVESTIGATION_NAME} investigation.

## Your Assigned Claims
{CLAIM_SET}

## Evidence Baseline
Read evidence from: {EVIDENCE_PATH}

## Your Task
Investigate your assigned claims using {EVIDENCE_STANDARDS}.

## Output
Write findings to: {OUTPUT_PATH}

## Output Format
[structured template...]
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Agent Roles, Evidence Passing Protocol)

before.title: T204.3: State Management Design
before.description:
Design state management system for tracking investigation progress.

## DELIVERABLE
`schemas/consensus-research-state.schema.json` + implementation spec

## STATE FILE MUST TRACK
- current_phase: Which phase is active (1-4)
- phase_status: Per-phase completion status
- claims: Per-claim status and verdicts
- agents: Which agents have completed, their output paths
- gates: Gate status (pending, approved, rejected)
- questions: Collected questions with severity and status
- suspicious_consensus: Flagged unanimous agreements
- session: Current session ID, start time, last activity

## STATE TRANSITIONS
```
Phase 1: pending  active  evidence_collected  gate_1_pending  gate_1_approved
Phase 2: pending  agents_spawned  agents_complete  gate_2_pending  gate_2_approved
Phase 3: pending  synthesis_active  voting_complete  gate_3_pending  gate_3_approved
Phase 4: pending  artifacts_generating  complete  gate_4_approved
```

## PERSISTENCE
- State file: `.consensus-research/state.json`
- Atomic writes (temp file  validate  rename)
- Checksum for integrity
- Recovery from interrupted state

## INTEGRATION WITH CLAUDE-TODO
- Optionally create claude-todo tasks from state
- Sync state  task status
- Export state to TodoWrite format

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Completion Criteria, Position Identification)

before.title: T204.6: Error Recovery Protocols
before.description:
Define error handling and recovery protocols for framework robustness.

## DELIVERABLE
Error recovery section in spec + state recovery implementation

## ERROR SCENARIOS

### Agent Failure
- Worker agent returns error or incomplete output
- Mitigation: Retry with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts)
- Fallback: Mark claim as INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE, flag for HITL

### HITL Gate Timeout
- User doesn't respond within timeout period
- Mitigation: Reminder notification, extend timeout
- Fallback: Auto-proceed with Synthesis Agent recommendation (if configured)
- Alternative: Pause investigation, persist state for resume

### Session Interruption
- Context window exhausted mid-investigation
- Mitigation: State file persists all progress
- Recovery: New session reads state, resumes from last checkpoint
- Phase checkpoints: After each phase completion

### Conflicting Agent Outputs
- Two agents give contradictory evidence for same claim
- Mitigation: Challenge Agent reviews conflict
- Escalation: SUSPICIOUS_CONSENSUS or HITL gate

### Missing Evidence
- Phase 1 cannot find sufficient evidence for claims
- Mitigation: Flag claims as EVIDENCE_NEEDED
- Options: Proceed with available evidence, request more sources, abort claim

### State Corruption
- State file corrupted or invalid
- Mitigation: Checksum validation on read
- Recovery: Reconstruct from output files + claude-todo tasks
- Backup: Periodic state snapshots

## RECOVERY COMMANDS
```bash
claude-todo research --resume          # Resume from state file
claude-todo research --status          # Show current state
claude-todo research --recover         # Attempt state reconstruction
claude-todo research --abort           # Clean abort with state preservation
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Risk Mitigation, Session Recovery)

before.title: T204.7: Invocation Modes Specification
before.description:
Define different invocation modes for the framework.

## DELIVERABLE
CLI specification section with all modes documented

## INVOCATION MODES

### Interactive Mode (Default)
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus --config ./research.yaml
```
- Pauses at each HITL gate for user input
- Shows progress in real-time
- Allows mid-investigation adjustments

### Batch Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus --config ./research.yaml --batch
```
- Runs without HITL pauses (uses Synthesis Agent recommendations)
- Collects all questions for end-of-run review
- Suitable for overnight/unattended runs

### Dry Run Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus --config ./research.yaml --dry-run
```
- Validates config, shows execution plan
- Lists claims, agents, phases, gates
- No actual agent spawning

### Resume Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --resume [state-file]
```
- Resumes from persisted state
- Validates state integrity before resuming
- Shows what was completed, what remains

### Status Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --status [state-file]
```
- Shows current investigation status
- Phase progress, claim verdicts, pending gates
- No modification

### Single Phase Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --phase 2 --config ./research.yaml
```
- Run only specific phase
- Requires prior phase outputs to exist
- Useful for re-running failed phases

## CLI OPTIONS
| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| --config | Path to config YAML |
| --framework | Framework type (consensus, adversarial, etc.) |
| --batch | Run without HITL pauses |
| --dry-run | Validate and show plan only |
| --resume | Resume from state file |
| --status | Show investigation status |
| --phase N | Run specific phase only |
| --verbose | Detailed output |
| --output-dir | Override output directory |

## REFERENCES
- T204 parent task
- claude-todo existing CLI patterns

before.title: FEATURE: Multi-Phase Consensus Research Framework Plugin
before.description:
Build a reusable plugin/command for claude-todo that implements the Multi-Phase Consensus Research Framework. This would be a programmatically callable tool with prompt-style instructions for any project to use when investigating claims, validating features, or planning complex work.

## CONCEPT
A repeatable, structured process for rigorous multi-agent investigation with:
- Configurable phases (Discovery  Adversarial  Consolidation  Validation  Final)
- Agent role definitions (Advocate/Challenger/Arbiter pattern)
- Gate-based progression (scope approval, conflict resolution, archive approval, completeness sign-off)
- Evidence standards and consensus thresholds
- Artifact generation (reports, specs, corrections)

## PROPOSED STRUCTURE
| Round | Purpose | Agents | Gate |
|-------|---------|--------|------|
| Round 1 | Discovery & Inventory | 6 inventory agents | Gate 1: Scope approval |
| Round 2 | Adversarial Challenge | 15 (5 domains  Advocate/Challenger/Arbiter) | Gate 2: Conflict resolution |
| Round 3 | Consolidation | 7 merge agents | Gate 3: Archive approval |
| Round 4 | Validation | 3 fresh validation agents | Gate 4: Completeness sign-off |
| Round 5 | Final Spec | 2 finalization agents | Gate 5: Implementation GO |

## INVOCATION
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus \
  --claims 'claim1.md,claim2.md' \
  --evidence-dir ./evidence \
  --output-dir ./consensus-output \
  --agents 5 \
  --threshold 0.8
```

## KEY FEATURES
1. Prompt-driven agent deployment (subagent_type patterns)
2. Round-robin challenge protocol with configurable rounds
3. Evidence standards (reproducible tests, measurements, citations)
4. Consensus thresholds (4/5 default, configurable)
5. Artifact templates (report, feature spec, doc correction, evidence dossier)
6. Gate progression with approval checkpoints
7. Deadlock resolution mechanisms
8. Context handoff for long investigations

## IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Could be a Claude Code plugin or claude-todo subcommand
- Needs integration with Task tool for agent deployment
- Should support both interactive and batch modes
- Evidence dossiers should be machine-readable (JSON) and human-readable (MD)
- Consider Serena MCP integration for code analysis phases

## SUCCESS CRITERIA
- Repeatable: Same inputs produce consistent process
- Valuable output: Single consolidated report, not scattered docs
- Configurable: Adjust agents, rounds, thresholds per project
- Transparent: Full evidence trail for all decisions

before.title: CLAIM-01: jq 'Argument list too long' at >100 tasks

OWNED BY: Technical Validation Agent (T190). EVIDENCE NEEDED: Reproducible test case with 100+ tasks. TEST: Create large todo.json, run 'claude-todo list --format json | jq .tasks[]'. STANDARD: Same result in 3/3 runs OR code analysis confirms.

before.description: Adversarial validation phase. ROUND 1: Each agent presents findings (max 200 tokens/claim with evidence citations). before.description: VOTING RULES: PROVEN (4/5 agents + reproducible evidence), REFUTED (counter-evidence invalidates OR 2/5 agree), CONTESTED (3/5 split after 2 rounds). DEADLOCK: Implementation Agent runs tie-breaker test. If still deadlock, mark DEFERRED. Gate: All 10 claims classified, deadlocks resolved or documented.

before.title: FEATURE: Multi-Phase Consensus Research Framework Plugin
before.description:
Build a reusable plugin/command for claude-todo that implements the Multi-Phase Consensus Research Framework. This would be a programmatically callable tool with prompt-style instructions for any project to use when investigating claims, validating features, or planning complex work.

## CONCEPT
A repeatable, structured process for rigorous multi-agent investigation with:
- Configurable phases (Discovery  Adversarial  Consolidation  Validation  Final)
- Agent role definitions (Advocate/Challenger/Arbiter pattern)
- Gate-based progression (scope approval, conflict resolution, archive approval, completeness sign-off)
- Evidence standards and consensus thresholds
- Artifact generation (reports, specs, corrections)

## PROPOSED STRUCTURE
| Round | Purpose | Agents | Gate |
|-------|---------|--------|------|
| Round 1 | Discovery & Inventory | 6 inventory agents | Gate 1: Scope approval |
| Round 2 | Adversarial Challenge | 15 (5 domains  Advocate/Challenger/Arbiter) | Gate 2: Conflict resolution |
| Round 3 | Consolidation | 7 merge agents | Gate 3: Archive approval |
| Round 4 | Validation | 3 fresh validation agents | Gate 4: Completeness sign-off |
| Round 5 | Final Spec | 2 finalization agents | Gate 5: Implementation GO |

## INVOCATION
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus \
  --claims 'claim1.md,claim2.md' \
  --evidence-dir ./evidence \
  --output-dir ./consensus-output \
  --agents 5 \
  --threshold 0.8
```

## KEY FEATURES
1. Prompt-driven agent deployment (subagent_type patterns)
2. Round-robin challenge protocol with configurable rounds
3. Evidence standards (reproducible tests, measurements, citations)
4. Consensus thresholds (4/5 default, configurable)
5. Artifact templates (report, feature spec, doc correction, evidence dossier)
6. Gate progression with approval checkpoints
7. Deadlock resolution mechanisms
8. Context handoff for long investigations

## IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Could be a Claude Code plugin or claude-todo subcommand
- Needs integration with Task tool for agent deployment
- Should support both interactive and batch modes
- Evidence dossiers should be machine-readable (JSON) and human-readable (MD)
- Consider Serena MCP integration for code analysis phases

## SUCCESS CRITERIA
- Repeatable: Same inputs produce consistent process
- Valuable output: Single consolidated report, not scattered docs
- Configurable: Adjust agents, rounds, thresholds per project
- Transparent: Full evidence trail for all decisions

7. Deadlock resolution mechanisms
8. Context handoff for long investigations

## IMPLEMENTATION NOTES
- Could be a Claude Code plugin or claude-todo subcommand
- Needs integration with Task tool for agent deployment
- Should support both interactive and batch modes
- Evidence dossiers should be machine-readable (JSON) and human-readable (MD)
- Consider Serena MCP integration for code analysis phases

## SUCCESS CRITERIA
- Repeatable: Same inputs produce consistent process
- Valuable output: Single consolidated report, not scattered docs
- Configurable: Adjust agents, rounds, thresholds per project
- Transparent: Full evidence trail for all decisions

before.title: TESTING: Real-world production testing of v0.14.0 (NOT BATS)
before.description:
## Purpose
Validate v0.14.0 in real-world production scenarios, not just BATS unit tests.
BATS tests pass (1124/1124) but we need to verify:
1. Fresh project initialization works
2. Existing project migration/repair works
3. All commands function correctly in real usage
4. Edge cases that BATS may not cover

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Fresh Project Setup
```bash
mkdir /tmp/test-fresh && cd /tmp/test-fresh
claude-todo init
claude-todo add "Test task 1" --priority high --phase setup
claude-todo add "Test task 2" --depends T001
claude-todo focus set T001
claude-todo session start
# ... use for real work ...
claude-todo complete T001 --notes "Done"
claude-todo phase advance
claude-todo session end
```

### Scenario 2: Existing Project Migration
Test with a project that has:
- Old v2.1.0 format (string project field)
- Missing phases
- Wrong phase structure
```bash
claude-todo migrate status
claude-todo migrate run --auto
claude-todo migrate repair --dry-run
claude-todo migrate repair --auto
claude-todo validate
```

### Scenario 3: Complex Workflow
- Multiple tasks with dependencies
- Blocked tasks
- Archive operations
- Export to various formats
- Phase transitions

### Scenario 4: Edge Cases
- Very large task counts (100+ tasks)
- Special characters in titles/descriptions
- Concurrent operations
- Network disconnection during operations

### Scenario 5: Error Recovery
- Intentionally corrupt todo.json
- Run validate --fix
- Verify recovery works

## Success Criteria
- All scenarios complete without errors
- Data integrity maintained throughout
- No data loss in any scenario
- Commands produce expected output
- Help text is accurate and complete

## Deliverables
- Test report with pass/fail for each scenario
- List of any bugs discovered
- Recommendations for documentation updates

before.title: ENHANCEMENT: Consolidate fix/repair terminology and functionality into unified pattern
before.description:
## Problem Statement
The codebase currently uses inconsistent terminology and patterns for auto-fixing/repairing data:
1. `validate --fix` - Fixes checksum, focus mismatch, multiple active tasks
2. `migrate repair` - Fixes phase structure, _meta fields, focus fields
3. `init --update-claude-md` - Updates CLAUDE.md injection

This creates confusion about:
- When to use which command
- What each command actually fixes
- Flag conventions (--fix vs repair subcommand)

## Current State Analysis

### `validate --fix` capabilities:
- Updates checksum to match tasks array
- Fixes focus.currentTask to match active task
- Keeps only first active task (deactivates others)
- Updates CLAUDE.md injection if outdated

### `migrate repair` capabilities:
- Ensures canonical 5-phase structure
- Adds missing phases, removes obsolete phases
- Fixes phase ordering
- Ensures _meta has all required fields
- Ensures focus has all required fields
- Preserves existing status/timestamps

### Flag Convention Inconsistencies:
- Some commands use `--flag` (double dash): --fix, --dry-run, --auto
- Some commands use subcommands: repair, status, run
- No consistent pattern across CLI

## Proposed Solution

### Option A: Unified `--fix` flag
Add `--fix` flag to all relevant commands:
- `validate --fix` (existing)
- `migrate --fix` (instead of `migrate repair`)
- `init --fix` (fix project issues)

### Option B: Standalone `repair` command
Create `claude-todo repair` as top-level command:
- `repair --dry-run` - Preview all repairs
- `repair --auto` - Apply all repairs
- `repair phases` - Fix phases only
- `repair schema` - Fix schema compliance
- `repair checksum` - Fix checksums

### Option C: Hybrid (Recommended)
Keep `validate --fix` for validation-related fixes
Create `claude-todo repair` for structural repairs
Ensure both produce consistent output format

## Implementation Tasks (if approved)
1. Create lib/repair.sh with reusable repair interface
2. Refactor migrate repair to use lib/repair.sh
3. Add repair logic for other scenarios
4. Ensure consistent flag naming across CLI
5. Update documentation
6. Add integration tests for repair scenarios

## Decision Needed
- Which option to implement (A, B, or C)?
- Should --fix and repair both exist or consolidate?
- What is the "escape hatch" for users when things break?

before.title: DOCS: Create production readiness checklist and migration guide
before.description:
## Current State Assessment

### What Works (BATS tested):
- All 1124 tests passing
- init, add, update, complete, list, focus, session commands
- Phase tracking (phases, phase set/start/complete/advance)
- Migration v2.1.0  v2.2.0
- Repair command for fixing phase structure
- Validation with --fix
- Export, import, archive, backup commands

### What Needs Real-World Validation:
- Fresh install experience
- Existing project upgrade path
- Error recovery scenarios
- Performance with large datasets
- Multi-user/concurrent access

## Production Readiness Gaps

### 1. User Documentation Gap
Users don't know what to run when. Need clear guide:
- "I just installed claude-todo"  what to run?
- "I have an existing project"  what to run?
- "Something broke"  what to run?

### 2. Migration/Repair Clarity Gap
Current state is confusing:
- migrate run - upgrades schema version
- migrate repair - fixes structure within version
- validate --fix - fixes data integrity
- init --update-claude-md - updates instructions

Need: Single "make it work" command or clear flowchart

### 3. Error Messages Gap
Some error messages don't tell users what to do next.
Example: "Checksum mismatch" doesn't say "run validate --fix"

## Deliverables Needed

### 1. Quick Start Guide
For README.md:
```markdown
## Quick Start
# New project
claude-todo init

# Existing project (upgrade from older version)
claude-todo migrate run --auto
claude-todo migrate repair --auto
claude-todo validate --fix
```

### 2. Troubleshooting Guide
For docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md:
- Common errors and fixes
- Recovery procedures
- When to use which command

### 3. Migration Decision Tree
```
Is project initialized?
 No  claude-todo init
 Yes  Is version current?
          No  claude-todo migrate run --auto
          Yes  Are phases correct?
                   No  claude-todo migrate repair --auto
                   Yes  Run claude-todo validate
```

Users don't know what to run when. Need clear guide:
- "I just installed claude-todo"  what to run?
- "I have an existing project"  what to run?
- "Something broke"  what to run?

### 2. Migration/Repair Clarity Gap
Current state is confusing:
- migrate run - upgrades schema version
- migrate repair - fixes structure within version
- validate --fix - fixes data integrity
- init --update-claude-md - updates instructions

Need: Single "make it work" command or clear flowchart

### 3. Error Messages Gap
Some error messages don't tell users what to do next.
Example: "Checksum mismatch" doesn't say "run validate --fix"

## Deliverables Needed

### 1. Quick Start Guide
For README.md:
```markdown
## Quick Start
# New project
claude-todo init

# Existing project (upgrade from older version)
claude-todo migrate run --auto
claude-todo migrate repair --auto
claude-todo validate --fix
```

### 2. Troubleshooting Guide
For docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md:
- Common errors and fixes
- Recovery procedures
- When to use which command

### 3. Migration Decision Tree
```
Is project initialized?
 No  claude-todo init
 Yes  Is version current?
          No  claude-todo migrate run --auto
          Yes  Are phases correct?
                   No  claude-todo migrate repair --auto
                   Yes  Run claude-todo validate
```

after.description:
## EPIC: TodoWrite  claude-todo Bidirectional Sync Integration

Master tracking task for all TodoWrite sync integration work.

### Purpose
Seamless integration between durable claude-todo persistence and ephemeral TodoWrite session tracking with automatic injection at session start and extraction at session end.

### Completed Work

#### Research Phase (T227)
- T227: Research epic 
- T228: Analyze TodoWrite schema 
- T229: Design injection mechanism 
- T230: Design extraction strategy 
- T231: Metadata mapping analysis 
- T232: Conflict resolution protocol 
- T233: Hook integration architecture 

#### Implementation Phase
- T234: inject-todowrite.sh 
- T235: extract-todowrite.sh 
- T236: sync-todowrite.sh orchestrator 
- T237: Fix extraction tests 

#### Phase Enhancement
- T257: Inject with phase filtering 
- T258: Extract with phase inheritance 
- T259: Sync state format for phases 
- T265: phase-sync.bats tests 
- T272: sync.md documentation 
- T278: CLAUDE.md injection template 

### Remaining Work
- T291: Update phase-sync.bats fixtures to 5-phase structure
- T315: BUG: activeForm verb conjugation broken
- T321: Document sync --clear, --phase, --no-save-state options

### Commands Delivered
```bash
claude-todo sync --inject           # Prepare tasks for TodoWrite
claude-todo sync --inject --focused-only
claude-todo sync --extract <file>   # Merge TodoWrite state back
claude-todo sync --extract --dry-run
claude-todo sync --status           # Show sync session state
```

### Success Criteria
- [ ] All pending subtasks complete
- [ ] All tests passing with 5-phase fixtures
- [ ] Documentation complete for all sync options
- [ ] activeForm generation produces correct grammar

before.title: BUG: TodoWrite export activeForm verb conjugation broken
before.description:
## Problem
When exporting tasks to TodoWrite format, the activeForm generation produces incorrect verb conjugation:
```json
{
  "content": "Core feature A",
  "activeForm": "Coring feature A"  // Wrong - should be 'Working on Core feature A' or similar
}
{
  "content": "Testing task",
  "activeForm": "Testinging task"   // Double -ing suffix
}
```

## Root Cause
The verb conjugation logic in export.sh (or inject-todowrite.sh) naively appends '-ing' to first word without proper grammar rules.

## Solution Options
1. **Simple prefix**: Use 'Working on {title}' pattern for all tasks
2. **Smart conjugation**: Handle common verb patterns:
   - 'Test'  'Testing' (add -ing)
   - 'Core'  'Working on Core' (non-verb, use prefix)
   - Words ending in 'e'  drop 'e', add '-ing'
3. **User override**: Allow activeForm to be specified in task metadata

## Recommendation
Option 1 (simple prefix) is safest and most consistent. Grammar-aware conjugation is complex and error-prone.

## Related Work
Part of TodoWrite sync integration effort:
- T227: Research TodoWrite  claude-todo Bidirectional Sync Integration
- T234: Implement inject-todowrite.sh script
- T235: Implement extract-todowrite.sh script

## Found During
T311 v0.14.0 production testing

before.title: T204.1: Configuration Schema Design
before.description:
Design YAML/JSON configuration schema for reusable consensus framework plugin.

## DELIVERABLE
`schemas/consensus-research-config.schema.json`

## SCHEMA MUST DEFINE
- claims: Array of claim definitions (id, title, description, category)
- evidence_sources: Paths to evidence directories/files
- agents: Agent configuration (count, types, subagent_type mappings)
- thresholds: Consensus thresholds (proven, refuted, contested)
- output: Output directory and file naming conventions
- gates: HITL gate configuration (which gates, timeout, blocking)
- phases: Phase definitions (which phases to run, order)

## EXAMPLE CONFIG
```yaml
framework: consensus-research
version: 1.0
investigation:
  name: "claude-todo-claims"
  claims_file: ./claims.yaml
  evidence_dir: ./evidence
  output_dir: ./consensus-output
agents:
  worker_count: 5
  synthesis_type: project-supervisor-orchestrator
thresholds:
  proven: 0.8  # 4/5
  refuted: 0.4  # 2/5
gates:
  - after_phase: 1
    blocking: true
    timeout: 24h
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Execution Protocol section)
- T204 parent task for context

before.title: DOCS: Create production readiness checklist and migration guide
before.description:
## Current State Assessment

### What Works (BATS tested):
- All 1124 tests passing
- init, add, update, complete, list, focus, session commands
- Phase tracking (phases, phase set/start/complete/advance)
- Migration v2.1.0  v2.2.0
- Repair command for fixing phase structure
- Validation with --fix
- Export, import, archive, backup commands

### What Needs Real-World Validation:
- Fresh install experience
- Existing project upgrade path
- Error recovery scenarios
- Performance with large datasets
- Multi-user/concurrent access

## Production Readiness Gaps

### 1. User Documentation Gap
Users don't know what to run when. Need clear guide:
- "I just installed claude-todo"  what to run?
- "I have an existing project"  what to run?
- "Something broke"  what to run?

### 2. Migration/Repair Clarity Gap
Current state is confusing:
- migrate run - upgrades schema version
- migrate repair - fixes structure within version
- validate --fix - fixes data integrity
- init --update-claude-md - updates instructions

Need: Single "make it work" command or clear flowchart

### 3. Error Messages Gap
Some error messages don't tell users what to do next.
Example: "Checksum mismatch" doesn't say "run validate --fix"

## Deliverables Needed

### 1. Quick Start Guide
For README.md:
```markdown
## Quick Start
# New project
claude-todo init

# Existing project (upgrade from older version)
claude-todo migrate run --auto
claude-todo migrate repair --auto
claude-todo validate --fix
```

### 2. Troubleshooting Guide
For docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md:
- Common errors and fixes
- Recovery procedures
- When to use which command

### 3. Migration Decision Tree
```
Is project initialized?
 No  claude-todo init
 Yes  Is version current?
          No  claude-todo migrate run --auto
          Yes  Are phases correct?
                   No  claude-todo migrate repair --auto
                   Yes  Run claude-todo validate
```

before.title: EPIC: Hierarchy Enhancement Phase 1 - Core (v0.15.0)
before.description:
## Overview
Implement core hierarchical task management for claude-todo.

## Scope
- Schema v2.3.0 with type, parentId, size fields
- Migration from v2.2.0 (label conventions to schema fields)
- Validation rules (depth, siblings, parent existence, orphans)
- CLI flags: --type, --parent, --size, --tree, --children
- Anti-hallucination error messages

## Success Criteria
- All existing tests pass (1124+)
- New hierarchy tests pass (100+)
- Migration preserves all data
- Validation catches invalid operations

## NOT in scope (Phase 2)
- Auto-complete parent
- reparent/promote commands
- Tab completion
- Advanced automation

before.title: EPIC: Hierarchy Enhancement Phase 2 - Automation (v0.16.0)
before.description:
## Overview
Advanced automation and UX enhancements for hierarchy.

## Scope
- Auto-complete parent when all children done
- Auto-unblock when dependencies complete
- Orphan detection and repair commands
- tree, reparent, promote commands
- Tab completion for --parent
- Focus integration with hierarchy context
- next command hierarchy awareness

## Success Criteria
- All Phase 1 + Phase 2 tests pass
- Auto-behaviors work correctly
- Commands are intuitive and documented
- Performance acceptable for 500+ tasks

before.title: T328.1: Update todo.schema.json to v2.3.0 with type, parentId, size fields
before.description:
Add new optional fields to task definition:
- type: enum [epic, task, subtask], default task
- parentId: string or null, pattern ^T\d{3,}$
- size: enum [small, medium, large] or null

Update schema version to 2.3.0

before.title: T339.1: Implement parent auto-complete in complete-task.sh
before.description:
When completing a task:
1. Check if task has parent
2. Check if all siblings are done
3. If yes: auto-complete parent (configurable mode)

Config options in todo-config.json:
- hierarchy.autoCompleteParent: true|false
- hierarchy.autoCompleteMode: auto|suggest|off

Modes:
- auto: Complete parent silently
- suggest: Prompt user (default)
- off: No auto-complete

before.title: RESEARCH: Release Version Management System Design
before.description:
## Problem Statement
Current version label system (v0.15.0, v0.17.0, etc.) has issues:
1. Version numbers shift as patches and features change scope
2. Tasks get assigned to versions that never actually release those features
3. No tracking of what actually shipped in which version
4. Version labels get out of sync with actual releases
5. Hallucination/drift between planned vs actual release contents

## Research Questions
1. How should claude-todo track application versions vs task associations?
2. Should there be a 'release' entity separate from labels?
3. How to handle version bumps that move tasks between releases?
4. How to track what actually shipped vs what was planned?
5. How to support semver (major.minor.patch) semantics?

## Potential Solutions to Explore

### Option A: Release Entity
```json
{
  "releases": {
    "v0.19.0": {
      "status": "planned",  // planned, in-progress, released
      "targetDate": null,
      "releasedAt": null,
      "tasks": ["T429", "T430"],
      "changelog": "..."
    }
  }
}
```

### Option B: Task Release Field
```json
{
  "id": "T429",
  "plannedRelease": "v0.19.0",
  "actualRelease": null,  // Set when task ships
  "releaseHistory": ["v0.18.0", "v0.19.0"]  // If moved
}
```

### Option C: Release Branch Tracking
Integration with git tags/releases to sync actual versions.

## Deliverables
1. Design document comparing options
2. Schema proposal (if schema change needed)
3. CLI command proposals (release create, release add, release ship)
4. Integration with existing phase/label systems

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Clear recommendation for release tracking approach
- [ ] Schema design if applicable
- [ ] Understanding of semver handling
- [ ] Plan for migration from version labels

## NOT in Scope (for this research)
- Actual implementation
- Git integration details
- CI/CD automation

```json
      "changelog": "..."
    }
  }
}
```

### Option B: Task Release Field
```json
{
  "id": "T429",
  "plannedRelease": "v0.19.0",
  "actualRelease": null,  // Set when task ships
  "releaseHistory": ["v0.18.0", "v0.19.0"]  // If moved
}
```

### Option C: Release Branch Tracking
Integration with git tags/releases to sync actual versions.

## Deliverables
1. Design document comparing options
2. Schema proposal (if schema change needed)
3. CLI command proposals (release create, release add, release ship)
4. Integration with existing phase/label systems

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Clear recommendation for release tracking approach
- [ ] Schema design if applicable
- [ ] Understanding of semver handling
- [ ] Plan for migration from version labels

## NOT in Scope (for this research)
- Actual implementation
- Git integration details
- CI/CD automation

before.title: EPIC: TodoWrite Bidirectional Sync Integration
before.description:
## EPIC: TodoWrite  claude-todo Bidirectional Sync Integration

Master tracking task for all TodoWrite sync integration work.

### Purpose
Seamless integration between durable claude-todo persistence and ephemeral TodoWrite session tracking with automatic injection at session start and extraction at session end.

### Completed Work

#### Research Phase (T227)
- T227: Research epic 
- T228: Analyze TodoWrite schema 
- T229: Design injection mechanism 
- T230: Design extraction strategy 
- T231: Metadata mapping analysis 
- T232: Conflict resolution protocol 
- T233: Hook integration architecture 

#### Implementation Phase
- T234: inject-todowrite.sh 
- T235: extract-todowrite.sh 
- T236: sync-todowrite.sh orchestrator 
- T237: Fix extraction tests 

#### Phase Enhancement
- T257: Inject with phase filtering 
- T258: Extract with phase inheritance 
- T259: Sync state format for phases 
- T265: phase-sync.bats tests 
- T272: sync.md documentation 
- T278: CLAUDE.md injection template 

### Remaining Work
- T291: Update phase-sync.bats fixtures to 5-phase structure
- T315: BUG: activeForm verb conjugation broken
- T321: Document sync --clear, --phase, --no-save-state options

### Commands Delivered
```bash
claude-todo sync --inject           # Prepare tasks for TodoWrite
claude-todo sync --inject --focused-only
claude-todo sync --extract <file>   # Merge TodoWrite state back
claude-todo sync --extract --dry-run
claude-todo sync --status           # Show sync session state
```

### Success Criteria
- [ ] All pending subtasks complete
- [ ] All tests passing with 5-phase fixtures
- [ ] Documentation complete for all sync options
- [ ] activeForm generation produces correct grammar

- Remove migrated labels from labels array
- Update _meta.version to 2.3.0
- Preserve all existing data

before.title: EPIC: Smart Archive System Enhancement
before.description:
## Overview
Comprehensive enhancement of the archive system to be smarter, more automated, and relationship-aware.

## Current Limitations
- Age-only retention (no task importance awareness)
- No label filtering (can't exempt epics/important tasks)
- No relationship awareness (archives children while parent active)
- No dependency checking (breaks reference chains)
- Limited retrieval (archived = essentially lost)

## Proposed Solution
Three-tier implementation:
- Tier 1: Quick wins (exemptLabels, --exclude-labels, --safe flag, warnings)
- Tier 2: Smart defaults (labelPolicies, relationship-aware, unarchive, list --include-archive)
- Tier 3: Full automation (phase-triggered, cascade, interactive, analytics)

## Dependencies
- Requires Hierarchy Enhancement (T328/T339) for relationship-aware features
- Schema changes needed for archive metadata and config

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Epics/important tasks protected from auto-archive
- [ ] Archive respects parent-child and dependency relationships
- [ ] Archived tasks searchable and restorable
- [ ] Phase completion can trigger smart archiving
- [ ] Clear warnings before breaking references

Three-tier implementation:
- Tier 1: Quick wins (exemptLabels, --exclude-labels, --safe flag, warnings)
- Tier 2: Smart defaults (labelPolicies, relationship-aware, unarchive, list --include-archive)
- Tier 3: Full automation (phase-triggered, cascade, interactive, analytics)

## Dependencies
- Requires Hierarchy Enhancement (T328/T339) for relationship-aware features
- Schema changes needed for archive metadata and config

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Epics/important tasks protected from auto-archive
- [ ] Archive respects parent-child and dependency relationships
- [ ] Archived tasks searchable and restorable
- [ ] Phase completion can trigger smart archiving
- [ ] Clear warnings before breaking references

before.title: T429.4: Implement --safe flag with relationship checking
before.description:
Add --safe flag that prevents archiving tasks with active relationships.

## CLI Enhancement
```bash
archive --safe  # Don't break parent-child or dependency chains
```

## Relationship Checks
1. **Hierarchy check**: Don't archive if parentId points to active/pending task
2. **Dependency check**: Don't archive if other pending tasks depend on this task
3. **Child check**: Don't archive if task has non-archived children

## Implementation
1. Add --safe flag to archive.sh
2. Before archiving each task, run relationship checks
3. Requires lib/hierarchy.sh functions (depends on T328)
4. Excluded tasks shown with reason

## Output Example
```
 Safe mode exclusions:
  T045: Referenced by pending T328 (dependency)
  T089: Has active parent T088 (hierarchy)
  T102: Has 2 non-archived children

45 tasks archived, 3 excluded (safe mode)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] --safe prevents hierarchy-breaking archives
- [ ] --safe prevents dependency-breaking archives
- [ ] Clear output showing why tasks excluded
- [ ] Works with --dry-run

before.title: T429.5: Add archive warnings before breaking references
before.description:
Add pre-archive analysis that warns about reference-breaking operations.

## Behavior (Default)
Before archiving, analyze and warn:
```
 Archive Warning:
  T045 is referenced by pending T328 (dependency)
  T089 has active parent T088 (hierarchy)
  T102 has 2 pending children

  3 tasks will break references if archived.
  
Options:
  --safe     Exclude problematic tasks
  --force    Archive anyway (breaks references)
  
Archiving 45 tasks (3 with warnings). Continue? (y/N)
```

## Implementation
1. Run relationship analysis before archive
2. Categorize tasks: safe, warning, exempt
3. Show warning summary
4. Prompt for confirmation (unless --auto or --force)
5. Log warnings in archive operation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Warnings shown before confirmation
- [ ] Warning count in summary
- [ ] --force bypasses warnings
- [ ] --safe excludes warned tasks
- [ ] Warnings logged to todo-log.json

before.title: T339.1: Implement parent auto-complete in complete-task.sh
before.description:
When completing a task:
1. Check if task has parent
2. Check if all siblings are done
3. If yes: auto-complete parent (configurable mode)

Config options in todo-config.json:
- hierarchy.autoCompleteParent: true|false
- hierarchy.autoCompleteMode: auto|suggest|off

Modes:
- auto: Complete parent silently
- suggest: Prompt user (default)
- off: No auto-complete

before.title: BUG: Allow metadata updates on completed tasks (type, parentId, labels, size)
before.description:
update-task.sh blocks ALL updates to done tasks (line 617-626). But metadata fields are corrections, not work changes.

CURRENT BEHAVIOR:
```bash
if [[ "$CURRENT_STATUS" == "done" ]]; then
  output_error "Cannot update completed task"
  exit
fi
```

PROPOSED FIX:
Allow updates to metadata-only fields on completed tasks:
- type (categorization: taskepic)
- parentId (hierarchy structure)
- labels (tagging)
- size (estimation correction)

BLOCK changes to:
- status (can't un-complete)
- title/description (historical record)
- completedAt (audit trail)

RATIONALE:
Changing T328 from type=task to type=epic is a classification correction, not changing what work was done. Same for hierarchy restructuring.

IMPLEMENTATION:
Add whitelist check after the done check:
```bash
METADATA_ONLY_FIELDS=(type parentId labels size)
if [[ "$CURRENT_STATUS" == "done" ]]; then
  # Check if only metadata fields are being updated
  if only_metadata_fields_changed; then
    log_info "Allowing metadata correction on completed task"
  else
    output_error "Cannot update work fields on completed task"
    exit
  fi
fi
```


PROPOSED FIX:
Allow updates to metadata-only fields on completed tasks:
- type (categorization: taskepic)
- parentId (hierarchy structure)
- labels (tagging)
- size (estimation correction)

BLOCK changes to:
- status (can't un-complete)
- title/description (historical record)
- completedAt (audit trail)

RATIONALE:
Changing T328 from type=task to type=epic is a classification correction, not changing what work was done. Same for hierarchy restructuring.

IMPLEMENTATION:
Add whitelist check after the done check:
```bash
METADATA_ONLY_FIELDS=(type parentId labels size)
if [[ "$CURRENT_STATUS" == "done" ]]; then
  # Check if only metadata fields are being updated
  if only_metadata_fields_changed; then
    log_info "Allowing metadata correction on completed task"
  else
    output_error "Cannot update work fields on completed task"
    exit
  fi
fi
```

before.title: ENHANCE: Make sibling limit configurable in todo-config.json
before.description:
HIERARCHY-ENHANCEMENT-SPEC.md sets max 7 siblings based on Miller's 72 cognitive load research.

PROBLEM:
- 7 is appropriate for active work (human working memory)
- Too restrictive for organizing completed work or large epics
- Hit limit trying to set parentId on T347-T349 (T339 children)
- Hit limit on T390-T396 (T382 already has 7 children)

PROPOSED FIX:
Add to todo-config.json:
```json
{
  "hierarchy": {
    "maxSiblings": 7,
    "maxDepth": 3,
    "allowMetadataUpdateOnDone": true
  }
}
```

SPEC UPDATE:
Update HIERARCHY-ENHANCEMENT-SPEC.md to note:
- Default 7 is recommended for cognitive load
- Configurable for projects with larger task groups
- Document trade-off: larger groups = harder to track manually

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Add hierarchy.* config keys to CONFIG-SYSTEM-SPEC.md
2. Update lib/hierarchy.sh to read from config
3. Update add-task.sh/update-task.sh sibling checks

before.title: BUG: Allow metadata updates on completed tasks (type, parentId, labels, size)
before.description:
update-task.sh blocks ALL updates to done tasks (line 617-626). But metadata fields are corrections, not work changes.

CURRENT BEHAVIOR:
```bash
if [[ "$CURRENT_STATUS" == "done" ]]; then
  output_error "Cannot update completed task"
  exit
fi
```

PROPOSED FIX:
Allow updates to metadata-only fields on completed tasks:
- type (categorization: taskepic)
- parentId (hierarchy structure)
- labels (tagging)
- size (estimation correction)

BLOCK changes to:
- status (can't un-complete)
- title/description (historical record)
- completedAt (audit trail)

RATIONALE:
Changing T328 from type=task to type=epic is a classification correction, not changing what work was done. Same for hierarchy restructuring.

IMPLEMENTATION:
Add whitelist check after the done check:
```bash
METADATA_ONLY_FIELDS=(type parentId labels size)
if [[ "$CURRENT_STATUS" == "done" ]]; then
  # Check if only metadata fields are being updated
  if only_metadata_fields_changed; then
    log_info "Allowing metadata correction on completed task"
  else
    output_error "Cannot update work fields on completed task"
    exit
  fi
fi
```

before.title: T339.1: Implement parent auto-complete in complete-task.sh
before.description:
When completing a task:
1. Check if task has parent
2. Check if all siblings are done
3. If yes: auto-complete parent (configurable mode)

Config options in todo-config.json:
- hierarchy.autoCompleteParent: true|false
- hierarchy.autoCompleteMode: auto|suggest|off

Modes:
- auto: Complete parent silently
- suggest: Prompt user (default)
- off: No auto-complete

before.title: T339.4: Create scripts/reparent.sh command
before.description:
New reparent command:
- claude-todo reparent TXXX --to TYYY

Validates:
- Source task exists
- Target parent exists
- Target has valid type (not subtask)
- Move doesn't exceed depth limit
- Move doesn't create circular reference
- Target doesn't exceed sibling limit

Logs operation to todo-log.json

before.title: EPIC: Smart Archive System Enhancement
before.description:
## Overview
Comprehensive enhancement of the archive system to be smarter, more automated, and relationship-aware.

## Current Limitations
- Age-only retention (no task importance awareness)
- No label filtering (can't exempt epics/important tasks)
- No relationship awareness (archives children while parent active)
- No dependency checking (breaks reference chains)
- Limited retrieval (archived = essentially lost)

## Proposed Solution
Three-tier implementation:
- Tier 1: Quick wins (exemptLabels, --exclude-labels, --safe flag, warnings)
- Tier 2: Smart defaults (labelPolicies, relationship-aware, unarchive, list --include-archive)
- Tier 3: Full automation (phase-triggered, cascade, interactive, analytics)

## Dependencies
- Requires Hierarchy Enhancement (T328/T339) for relationship-aware features
- Schema changes needed for archive metadata and config

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Epics/important tasks protected from auto-archive
- [ ] Archive respects parent-child and dependency relationships
- [ ] Archived tasks searchable and restorable
- [ ] Phase completion can trigger smart archiving
- [ ] Clear warnings before breaking references

before.title: T204.1: Configuration Schema Design
before.description:
Design YAML/JSON configuration schema for reusable consensus framework plugin.

## DELIVERABLE
`schemas/consensus-research-config.schema.json`

## SCHEMA MUST DEFINE
- claims: Array of claim definitions (id, title, description, category)
- evidence_sources: Paths to evidence directories/files
- agents: Agent configuration (count, types, subagent_type mappings)
- thresholds: Consensus thresholds (proven, refuted, contested)
- output: Output directory and file naming conventions
- gates: HITL gate configuration (which gates, timeout, blocking)
- phases: Phase definitions (which phases to run, order)

## EXAMPLE CONFIG
```yaml
framework: consensus-research
version: 1.0
investigation:
  name: "claude-todo-claims"
  claims_file: ./claims.yaml
  evidence_dir: ./evidence
  output_dir: ./consensus-output
agents:
  worker_count: 5
  synthesis_type: project-supervisor-orchestrator
thresholds:
  proven: 0.8  # 4/5
  refuted: 0.4  # 2/5
gates:
  - after_phase: 1
    blocking: true
    timeout: 24h
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Execution Protocol section)
- T204 parent task for context

before.title: T204.2: Parameterized Prompt Templates
before.description:
Create parameterized prompt templates for all 6 agent types.

## DELIVERABLE
`templates/consensus-research/` directory with:
- agent-technical.prompt.md
- agent-design.prompt.md
- agent-docs.prompt.md
- agent-impl.prompt.md
- agent-challenge.prompt.md
- agent-synthesis.prompt.md

## TEMPLATE VARIABLES
Each template must use placeholders:
- `{INVESTIGATION_NAME}` - Name of the investigation
- `{CLAIM_SET}` - List of claims assigned to this agent
- `{EVIDENCE_PATH}` - Path to phase 1 evidence file
- `{OUTPUT_PATH}` - Path where agent writes findings
- `{EVIDENCE_STANDARDS}` - Standards for this claim type
- `{CONSENSUS_THRESHOLD}` - Voting threshold
- `{OTHER_AGENT_OUTPUTS}` - Paths to other agent files (for synthesis)

## EXAMPLE TEMPLATE
```markdown
# {AGENT_ROLE} Agent

You are the {AGENT_ROLE} for the {INVESTIGATION_NAME} investigation.

## Your Assigned Claims
{CLAIM_SET}

## Evidence Baseline
Read evidence from: {EVIDENCE_PATH}

## Your Task
Investigate your assigned claims using {EVIDENCE_STANDARDS}.

## Output
Write findings to: {OUTPUT_PATH}

## Output Format
[structured template...]
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Agent Roles, Evidence Passing Protocol)

before.title: T204.3: State Management Design
before.description:
Design state management system for tracking investigation progress.

## DELIVERABLE
`schemas/consensus-research-state.schema.json` + implementation spec

## STATE FILE MUST TRACK
- current_phase: Which phase is active (1-4)
- phase_status: Per-phase completion status
- claims: Per-claim status and verdicts
- agents: Which agents have completed, their output paths
- gates: Gate status (pending, approved, rejected)
- questions: Collected questions with severity and status
- suspicious_consensus: Flagged unanimous agreements
- session: Current session ID, start time, last activity

## STATE TRANSITIONS
```
Phase 1: pending  active  evidence_collected  gate_1_pending  gate_1_approved
Phase 2: pending  agents_spawned  agents_complete  gate_2_pending  gate_2_approved
Phase 3: pending  synthesis_active  voting_complete  gate_3_pending  gate_3_approved
Phase 4: pending  artifacts_generating  complete  gate_4_approved
```

## PERSISTENCE
- State file: `.consensus-research/state.json`
- Atomic writes (temp file  validate  rename)
- Checksum for integrity
- Recovery from interrupted state

## INTEGRATION WITH CLAUDE-TODO
- Optionally create claude-todo tasks from state
- Sync state  task status
- Export state to TodoWrite format

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Completion Criteria, Position Identification)

```json
      },

      "verdict": "REFUTED",
```

Structured output format for worker agents: claims addressed, evidence found, verdict per claim, confidence level, citations

All outputs should generate both:
- .json (machine-readable, for state tracking)
- .md (human-readable, for review)

before.title: T204.6: Error Recovery Protocols
before.description:
Define error handling and recovery protocols for framework robustness.

## DELIVERABLE
Error recovery section in spec + state recovery implementation

## ERROR SCENARIOS

### Agent Failure
- Worker agent returns error or incomplete output
- Mitigation: Retry with exponential backoff (max 3 attempts)
- Fallback: Mark claim as INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE, flag for HITL

### HITL Gate Timeout
- User doesn't respond within timeout period
- Mitigation: Reminder notification, extend timeout
- Fallback: Auto-proceed with Synthesis Agent recommendation (if configured)
- Alternative: Pause investigation, persist state for resume

### Session Interruption
- Context window exhausted mid-investigation
- Mitigation: State file persists all progress
- Recovery: New session reads state, resumes from last checkpoint
- Phase checkpoints: After each phase completion

### Conflicting Agent Outputs
- Two agents give contradictory evidence for same claim
- Mitigation: Challenge Agent reviews conflict
- Escalation: SUSPICIOUS_CONSENSUS or HITL gate

### Missing Evidence
- Phase 1 cannot find sufficient evidence for claims
- Mitigation: Flag claims as EVIDENCE_NEEDED
- Options: Proceed with available evidence, request more sources, abort claim

### State Corruption
- State file corrupted or invalid
- Mitigation: Checksum validation on read
- Recovery: Reconstruct from output files + claude-todo tasks
- Backup: Periodic state snapshots

## RECOVERY COMMANDS
```bash
claude-todo research --resume          # Resume from state file
claude-todo research --status          # Show current state
claude-todo research --recover         # Attempt state reconstruction
claude-todo research --abort           # Clean abort with state preservation
```

## REFERENCES
- claudedocs/CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC.md (Risk Mitigation, Session Recovery)

before.title: T204.7: Invocation Modes Specification
before.description:
Define different invocation modes for the framework.

## DELIVERABLE
CLI specification section with all modes documented

## INVOCATION MODES

### Interactive Mode (Default)
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus --config ./research.yaml
```
- Pauses at each HITL gate for user input
- Shows progress in real-time
- Allows mid-investigation adjustments

### Batch Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus --config ./research.yaml --batch
```
- Runs without HITL pauses (uses Synthesis Agent recommendations)
- Collects all questions for end-of-run review
- Suitable for overnight/unattended runs

### Dry Run Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --framework consensus --config ./research.yaml --dry-run
```
- Validates config, shows execution plan
- Lists claims, agents, phases, gates
- No actual agent spawning

### Resume Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --resume [state-file]
```
- Resumes from persisted state
- Validates state integrity before resuming
- Shows what was completed, what remains

### Status Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --status [state-file]
```
- Shows current investigation status
- Phase progress, claim verdicts, pending gates
- No modification

### Single Phase Mode
```bash
claude-todo research --phase 2 --config ./research.yaml
```
- Run only specific phase
- Requires prior phase outputs to exist
- Useful for re-running failed phases

## CLI OPTIONS
| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| --config | Path to config YAML |
| --framework | Framework type (consensus, adversarial, etc.) |
| --batch | Run without HITL pauses |
| --dry-run | Validate and show plan only |
| --resume | Resume from state file |
| --status | Show investigation status |
| --phase N | Run specific phase only |
| --verbose | Detailed output |
| --output-dir | Override output directory |

## REFERENCES
- T204 parent task
- claude-todo existing CLI patterns

before.title: Create lib/child-handler.sh strategy pattern

Open/Closed principle - new strategies addable without modifying existing.

before.title: EPIC: Task Decomposition System Implementation (TASK-DECOMPOSITION-SPEC v1.0.0)
before.description:
Master Epic for implementing the Task Decomposition System per TASK-DECOMPOSITION-SPEC.md v1.0.0. This implements Stage 4 (DECOMPOSE) of the RCSD Pipeline.

## CRITICAL SUBAGENT REQUIREMENTS

**ALL subagents working on Phase tasks and subtasks MUST:**
1. Update task notes regularly with progress details
2. Document what was completed in each work session
3. Record any issues, blockers, or unexpected findings
4. Note any deviations from the spec with rationale
5. Update notes BEFORE ending session for handoff

**Note Update Pattern:**
```bash
ct update <task-id> --notes "Session <date>: <what was done>"
ct update <task-id> --notes "BLOCKER: <issue description>"
ct update <task-id> --notes "COMPLETE: <summary of deliverables>"
```

## Implementation Phases

1. **Phase 1: Core Infrastructure** - Create lib/decomposition.sh, lib/llm-invoke.sh, scripts/decompose.sh
2. **Phase 2: Decomposition Pipeline** - Implement analyze_scope(), decompose_goals(), build_dependency_graph(), specify_tasks()
3. **Phase 3: Challenge System** - Challenge agent integration per CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC
4. **Phase 4: Dependency Detection** - Explicit, data flow, file conflict, semantic detection
5. **Phase 5: HITL Integration** - Human-in-the-loop gates and AskUserQuestion integration
6. **Phase 6: Schema Extensions** - Add decompositionId, atomicityScore, acceptance fields
7. **Phase 7: Testing** - Unit, integration, challenge, performance tests
8. **Phase 8: Documentation** - QUICK-REFERENCE, TODO_Task_Management, user guide

## Dependencies
- Requires: HIERARCHY-ENHANCEMENT-SPEC (type, parentId, size)
- Requires: LLM-AGENT-FIRST-SPEC (JSON output, exit codes)
- Requires: CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC (challenge protocol)
- Requires: LLM-TASK-ID-SYSTEM-DESIGN-SPEC (task ID format)

## Key Specifications
- Spec: docs/specs/TASK-DECOMPOSITION-SPEC.md
- Report: docs/specs/TASK-DECOMPOSITION-SPEC-IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT.md
- Target: v0.22.0+

after.title: EPIC: Task Decomposition System Implementation (TASK-DECOMPOSITION-SPEC v1.0.0)
after.description:
Master Epic for implementing the Task Decomposition System per TASK-DECOMPOSITION-SPEC.md v1.0.0. This implements Stage 4 (DECOMPOSE) of the RCSD Pipeline.

## CRITICAL SUBAGENT REQUIREMENTS

**ALL subagents working on Phase tasks and subtasks MUST:**
1. Update task notes regularly with progress details
2. Document what was completed in each work session
3. Record any issues, blockers, or unexpected findings
4. Note any deviations from the spec with rationale
5. Update notes BEFORE ending session for handoff

**Note Update Pattern:**
```bash
ct update <task-id> --notes "Session <date>: <what was done>"
ct update <task-id> --notes "BLOCKER: <issue description>"
ct update <task-id> --notes "COMPLETE: <summary of deliverables>"
```

## Implementation Phases

1. **Phase 1: Core Infrastructure** - Create lib/decomposition.sh, lib/llm-invoke.sh, scripts/decompose.sh
2. **Phase 2: Decomposition Pipeline** - Implement analyze_scope(), decompose_goals(), build_dependency_graph(), specify_tasks()
3. **Phase 3: Challenge System** - Challenge agent integration per CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC
4. **Phase 4: Dependency Detection** - Explicit, data flow, file conflict, semantic detection
5. **Phase 5: HITL Integration** - Human-in-the-loop gates and AskUserQuestion integration
6. **Phase 6: Schema Extensions** - Add decompositionId, atomicityScore, acceptance fields
7. **Phase 7: Testing** - Unit, integration, challenge, performance tests
8. **Phase 8: Documentation** - QUICK-REFERENCE, TODO_Task_Management, user guide

## Dependencies
- Requires: HIERARCHY-ENHANCEMENT-SPEC (type, parentId, size)
- Requires: LLM-AGENT-FIRST-SPEC (JSON output, exit codes)
- Requires: CONSENSUS-FRAMEWORK-SPEC (challenge protocol)
- Requires: LLM-TASK-ID-SYSTEM-DESIGN-SPEC (task ID format)

## Key Specifications
- Spec: docs/specs/TASK-DECOMPOSITION-SPEC.md
- Report: docs/specs/TASK-DECOMPOSITION-SPEC-IMPLEMENTATION-REPORT.md
- Target: v0.22.0+
